summary: Problem q1 asks to prove equivalence (mutual absolute continuity) of the Φ⁴₃ measure under a smooth, nonzero shift T_ψ on the 3-torus — a frontier quasi-invariance result of Cameron–Martin type. The current state shows no solution.tex was produced; the only solve attempt errored out interactively, and the best 'proof' carries 100 open issues with no verified content. The 19% solvability score reflects the deep stochastic-analysis machinery (renormalization, paracontrolled/regularity-structures estimates) required for a complete argument.
problems: No solution.tex artifact exists — the interactive solve attempt (claude-opus-4-8) terminated with an error before producing any proof.

The core mathematical blocker is controlling the Radon–Nikodym derivative under the shift: the Φ⁴₃ density involves renormalized quartic/quadratic terms whose change under a smooth shift produces ill-defined (divergence-requiring) cross terms that must be renormalized and shown to be a.s. finite.
The 'best proof' is effectively empty/unverified — flagged with 100 open issues, no clear experiment lineage, and no model output that can be reviewed for correctness.

No explicit construction of the Cameron–Martin direction is present: ψ is smooth so it should lie in the relevant Sobolev space, but the argument linking smoothness of ψ to equivalence (rather than mere quasi-invariance) is not laid out.

Lack of intermediate lemmas: missing the standard pipeline (Girsanov/Cameron–Martin shift on the Gaussian free field reference measure, control of the shifted interaction potential, uniform integrability of the exponential density).
highlights: The expected answer is known and affirmative (the measures are equivalent), giving a clear target and a well-understood Cameron–Martin-type proof strategy to aim for.
The problem is sharply and correctly stated (T³, μ = Φ⁴₃, smooth nonzero ψ), so there is no ambiguity in what must be proved.
The smoothness of ψ is a favorable assumption: it places the shift safely inside the Cameron–Martin space, which is the key enabler for an equivalence (not just singularity) result.
The prior issue is marked resolved, indicating the question scaffolding is in place even though the substantive proof is not.

suggested_todos.title: Diagnose and fix the interactive solve error (claude-opus-4-8) and re-run to produce an initial solution.tex skeleton

Draft the proof spine: express μ via the Gaussian free field reference measure, apply Cameron–Martin shift for smooth ψ, and reduce equivalence to integrability of the renormalized interaction density
Write the key lemma controlling renormalized cross/quadratic terms generated by the shift (Wick renormalization, a.s. finiteness, L^p bounds on the Radon–Nikodym derivative)
Verify ψ lies in the Cameron–Martin / appropriate Sobolev space and document why smoothness yields equivalence rather than singularity
Triage the 100 open issues on the best proof, discard empty/placeholder ones, and consolidate into a small set of substantive mathematical gaps
Add references to known quasi-invariance results for Φ⁴ measures (regularity structures / paracontrolled distributions literature) to anchor the renormalization estimates
